description_long: This bungalow features a wide, low-pitched hip roof covered in thatch, giving it a rustic and tropical appearance. The structure is primarily constructed from wood, with a large, open front porch supported by wooden posts and railings. The front facade is open and inviting, with a central entrance and visible seating area, while the sides and rear are more enclosed with evenly spaced windows. The building sits on a rectangular, slightly raised platform with a grassy lawn surrounding it. The overall design emphasizes natural materials and open-air living, suitable for warm climates. The architectural style and materials suggest a blend of traditional and tropical influences, ideal for residential or leisure use in urban parks or neighborhoods.
description: A single-story wooden bungalow with a thatched hip roof, open front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement, set on a grassy platform. The design is rustic and tropical, emphasizing natural materials and open-air living.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open porch, central entrance, and visible seating area under the thatched roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the enclosed wooden walls and evenly spaced windows, with the hip roof extending over the porch.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back entrance and symmetrical window arrangement, with the roof's full span visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the wooden siding and window pattern under the extended roof.
